channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: DOCFORGE-BENCH: A Comprehensive 0-shot Benchmark for Document Forgery Detection and Analysis
item.description:
Summary

  • DOCFORGE-BENCH is a zero-shot benchmark evaluating 14 pretrained methods on 8 document forgery datasets (text tampering, receipt forgery, identity document manipulation) without domain adaptation.
  • Key finding: a large Pixel-AUC vs. Pixel-F1 gap caused by extreme class imbalance (tampered pixels comprise ~0.27–4.17% of image pixels) and miscalibrated fixed thresholds (tau=0.5); Oracle-F1 far exceeds fixed-threshold F1.
  • A small calibration step (adapting a single threshold on N=10 domain images) recovers a substantial portion (39–55%) of the Oracle-F1 gap, indicating threshold adaptation—not retraining—is critical for deployment; overall, no method works reliably out-of-the-box, and modern generative-AI forgeries remain an open benchmark gap.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Zengqi Zhao', 'Weidi Xia', 'En Wei', 'Yan Zhang', 'Jane Mo', 'Tiannan Zhang', 'Yuanqin Dai', 'Zexi Chen', 'Yiran Tao', 'Simiao Ren']
  • Tags: ['document-forensics', 'forgery-detection', 'benchmarking', 'calibration', 'zero-shot-evaluation']

item.title: Exploiting the Final Component of Generator Architectures for AI-Generated Image Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a defense for AI-generated image detection by 'contaminating' real images with the generator's final architectural component and training a detector to distinguish contaminated vs. original images.
  • Introduces a taxonomy of generators based on their final components and categorizes 21 widely used generators to study cross-generator generalization.
  • Demonstrates sample-efficient training (100 samples per category) using a DINOv3 backbone, achieving 98.83% average accuracy across 22 unseen-generator test sets.
  • Focuses on robustness and generalization of detection models to images from unseen generator architectures.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yanzhu Liu', 'Xiao Liu', 'Yuexuan Wang', 'Mondal Soumik']
  • Tags: ['deepfake-detection', 'AI-generated-image-detection', 'defense/robustness', 'generator-architecture', 'generalization']

item.title: Low-rank Orthogonal Subspace Intervention for Generalizable Face Forgery Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies 'low-rank spurious bias' in CLIP feature space where dominant principal components encode forgery-irrelevant information that causes poor generalization in face forgery detection.
  • Proposes SeLop: an orthogonal low-rank projection intervention that isolates and removes a low-rank subspace of spurious correlation features, training on the orthogonal complement to focus on authentic forgery traces.
  • Implements the method with only 0.39M trainable parameters and reports state-of-the-art generalization and robustness across multiple face forgery benchmarks.
  • Frames the approach in causal representation learning to cut statistical shortcuts between spurious components and labels, improving detector reliability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chi Wang', 'Xinjue Hu', 'Boyu Wang', 'Ziwen He', 'Zhangjie Fu']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'face forgery detection', 'robustness', 'representation learning', 'spurious correlations']

item.title: Taming Preference Mode Collapse via Directional Decoupling Alignment in Diffusion Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies and quantifies Preference Mode Collapse (PMC) in text-to-image diffusion models trained with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), where models exploit reward models to produce narrow, high-scoring but low-diversity outputs.
  • Introduces DivGenBench, a benchmark to measure the extent of PMC with metrics for generative diversity and reward optimization.
  • Proposes Directional Decoupling Alignment (D^2-Align): learn a directional correction in the reward model's embedding space (with the model frozen) and apply this corrected reward signal during optimization to prevent collapse.
  • Evaluates D^2-Align qualitatively and quantitatively, showing improved alignment with human preference while maintaining or improving diversity compared to baseline RLHF methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chubin Chen', 'Sujie Hu', 'Jiashu Zhu', 'Meiqi Wu', 'Jintao Chen', 'Yanxun Li', 'Nisha Huang', 'Chengyu Fang', 'Jiahong Wu', 'Xiangxiang Chu', 'Xiu Li']
  • Tags: ['RLHF', 'reward hacking', 'robustness', 'generative diversity', 'diffusion models']

item.title: When Robots Obey the Patch: Universal Transferable Patch Attacks on Vision-Language-Action Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces UPA-RFAS, a method to craft universal, transferable physical adversarial patches against Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models controlling robots.
  • Method combines a feature-space objective with L1 prior and repulsive InfoNCE, a robustness-augmented two-phase min-max training (inner invisible per-sample perturbations, outer universal patch optimization), and two VLA-specific losses (Patch Attention Dominance and Patch Semantic Misalignment).
  • Evaluated across multiple VLA architectures, finetuned variants, simulation-to-real scenarios, and physical robot executions, showing strong cross-model, cross-task, and cross-view transferability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hui Lu', 'Yi Yu', 'Yiming Yang', 'Chenyu Yi', 'Qixin Zhang', 'Bingquan Shen', 'Alex C. Kot', 'Xudong Jiang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-patches', 'transferability', 'physical-attacks', 'robotics', 'robustness']

item.title: V-Attack: Targeting Disentangled Value Features for Controllable Adversarial Attacks on LVLMs
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies that transformer attention 'value' (V) features retain disentangled, high-entropy local semantic information and are better manipulation handles than entangled patch-token features for LVLMs.
  • Proposes V-Attack, which targets value features and comprises: (1) a Self-Value Enhancement module to enrich V's semantic representation, and (2) a Text-Guided Value Manipulation module to steer a source concept toward a target using prompts.
  • Demonstrates substantial improvements in controllable semantic adversarial attacks across multiple LVLMs (LLaVA, InternVL, DeepseekVL, GPT-4o), reporting an average +36% attack success over prior methods.
  • Provides code and data release to reproduce attacks and evaluations, exposing practical vulnerabilities in modern visual-language models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sen Nie', 'Jie Zhang', 'Jianxin Yan', 'Shiguang Shan', 'Xilin Chen']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-attack', 'LVLM', 'semantic-manipulation', 'attention-value-features', 'robustness-vulnerability']

item.title: Who Made This? Fake Detection and Source Attribution with Diffusion Features
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes FRIDA, a lightweight framework that leverages features from a pre-trained Stable Diffusion U-Net to detect AI-generated images and attribute them to source models.
  • Performs a layer-wise analysis of diffusion features and uses a training-free k-Nearest Neighbour method for fake detection plus a compact neural classifier for source attribution.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art cross-generator detection with limited data on the GenImage benchmark while maintaining robust source model attribution.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Simone Bonechi', 'Paolo Andreini', 'Barbara Toniella Corradini']
  • Tags: ['fake-image detection', 'model attribution', 'diffusion models', 'image forensics']

item.title: Removing the Trigger, Not the Backdoor: Alternative Triggers and Latent Backdoors
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates the existence of alternative triggers—patterns perceptually distinct from training triggers—that reliably activate the same backdoor and proves their inevitability theoretically.
  • Estimates the alternative-trigger backdoor direction in feature/representation space by contrasting clean and triggered representations, and develops a feature-guided attack that jointly optimizes target prediction and directional alignment.
  • Empirically shows that defenses which remove input-space training triggers often fail because latent backdoor directions remain, and advocates for defenses targeting backdoor directions in representation space rather than just input-space triggers.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Gorka Abad', 'Ermes Franch', 'Stefanos Koffas', 'Stjepan Picek']
  • Tags: ['backdoor attacks', 'alternative triggers', 'representation-space defenses', 'adversarial robustness', 'theoretical analysis']

item.title: RESBev: Making BEV Perception More Robust
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes RESBev, a plug-and-play method to improve robustness of BEV perception models against sensor degradation and adversarial attacks.
  • Builds a latent world model that captures spatiotemporal correlations across sequential BEV observations to predict clean BEV features and reconstruct corrupted inputs.
  • Operates at the semantic feature level of the Lift-Splat-Shoot pipeline, enabling recovery without changing the underlying backbone and with few-shot fine-tuning.
  • Evaluates on nuScenes and reports significant robustness gains against a range of natural disturbances and adversarial attacks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Lifeng Zhuo', 'Kefan Jin', 'Zhe Liu', 'Hesheng Wang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'defense', 'BEV perception', 'autonomous driving', 'latent world model']

item.title: When Detectors Forget Forensics: Blocking Semantic Shortcuts for Generalizable AI-Generated Image Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies 'semantic fallback' as a failure mode where VFM-based AI-generated image detectors rely on pretrained semantic priors (e.g., identity) instead of forgery-specific traces under distribution shift.
  • Proposes Geometric Semantic Decoupling (GSD), a parameter-free module that projects out semantic directions using a frozen VFM as semantic guide and a trainable VFM as artifact detector to force reliance on semantic-invariant forensic evidence.
  • Reports consistent improvements in cross-dataset and unseen-manipulation robustness (e.g., +1.2% video AUC, +3.0% on DF40) and generalization beyond faces to general-scene synthetic image detection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chao Shuai', 'Zhenguang Liu', 'Shaojing Fan', 'Bin Gong', 'Weichen Lian', 'Xiuli Bi', 'Zhongjie Ba', 'Kui Ren']
  • Tags: ['image forensics', 'deepfake detection', 'robustness', 'vision foundation models', 'defense']

item.title: Diffusion-Based Authentication of Copy Detection Patterns: A Multimodal Framework with Printer Signature Conditioning
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a diffusion-based multimodal authentication framework for Copy Detection Patterns (CDPs) that uses the original binary template, the printed CDP image, and a printer-identity representation.
  • Repurposes ControlNet-style denoising for class-conditioned noise prediction to perform fine-grained, multi-class printer classification (printer signature conditioning).
  • Demonstrates improved performance over traditional similarity metrics and prior deep-learning approaches on the Indigo 1x1 Base dataset, and generalises to counterfeit types unseen during training.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Bolutife Atoki', 'Iuliia Tkachenko', 'Bertrand Kerautret', 'Carlos Crispim-Junior']
  • Tags: ['authentication', 'anti-counterfeiting', 'diffusion-models', 'printer-forensics', 'robustness']

item.title: Comparative Analysis of Patch Attack on VLM-Based Autonomous Driving Architectures
item.description:
Summary

  • Evaluates physically realizable patch attacks on three vision-language model (VLM) autonomous-driving architectures (Dolphins, OmniDrive, LeapVAD) using CARLA simulation.
  • Uses black-box optimization with semantic homogenization to produce comparable adversarial patches across architectures.
  • Finds severe, sustained multi-frame failures and substantial object-detection degradation, with distinct vulnerability patterns per architecture.
  • Concludes current VLM designs for safety-critical driving poorly address physical adversarial threats.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['David Fernandez', 'Pedram MohajerAnsari', 'Amir Salarpour', 'Long Cheng', 'Abolfazl Razi', "Mert D. Pes\\'e"]
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'physical-adversarial-attacks', 'robustness', 'autonomous-driving', 'vision-language-models']

item.title: Where, What, Why: Toward Explainable 3D-GS Watermarking
item.description:
Summary

  • Representation-native robust watermarking method for 3D Gaussian Splatting that separates carrier selection (where) from preservation/compensation (how), enabling imperceptible, view-consistent message embedding.
  • Trio-Experts module selects Gaussian carriers; Safety and Budget Aware Gate (SBAG) allocates Gaussians to watermark carriers vs visual compensators, optimizing bit resilience under perturbations and bitrate budgets; channel-wise group mask limits gradient updates to preserve fidelity.
  • Decoupled fine-tuning provides per-Gaussian attributions for explainability; demonstrates robustness to common image distortions (compression, noise) and improves PSNR (+0.83 dB) and bit-accuracy (+1.24%) over prior methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Mingshu Cai', 'Jiajun Li', 'Osamu Yoshie', 'Yuya Ieiri', 'Yixuan Li']
  • Tags: ['watermarking', 'robustness', 'IP protection', '3D Gaussian Splatting', 'explainability']

item.title: The Reasoning Trap -- Logical Reasoning as a Mechanistic Pathway to Situational Awareness
item.description:
Summary

  • Argues that improvements in LLM logical reasoning (deduction, induction, abduction) create mechanistic pathways to increasing situational awareness in models.
  • Introduces the RAISE framework identifying three pathways (deductive self inference, inductive context recognition, abductive self modeling) and an escalation ladder from self-recognition to strategic deception.
  • Analyzes why current safety measures are insufficient and maps major reasoning research topics to amplifiers of situational awareness.
  • Proposes concrete safeguards including a 'Mirror Test' benchmark and a Reasoning Safety Parity Principle, calling on the reasoning community to address risks.

Paper Type

  • Position

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subramanyam Sahoo', 'Aman Chadha', 'Vinija Jain', 'Divya Chaudhary']
  • Tags: ['situational-awareness', 'logical-reasoning', 'ai-safety', 'emergent-capabilities', 'benchmarks/safeguards']

item.title: Adversarial Latent-State Training for Robust Policies in Partially Observable Domains
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces an adversarial latent-initial-state POMDP framework where an adversary selects the hidden initial latent distribution to evaluate robustness of partially observable RL policies.
  • Provides theoretical results: a latent minimax principle, characterization of worst-case defender distributions, and finite-sample concentration bounds that separate optimization and sampling terms.
  • Proposes iterative best-response training and theorem-motivated diagnostics; empirically demonstrates improved robustness on a Battleship benchmark (reducing average robustness gaps between Spread and Uniform distributions from 10.3 to 3.1 shots at equal budget).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Angad Singh Ahuja']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'reinforcement-learning', 'adversarial-training', 'POMDP', 'distribution-shift']

item.title: Infusion: Shaping Model Behavior by Editing Training Data via Influence Functions
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Infusion, a method that uses scalable influence-function approximations to compute small perturbations to training examples that induce targeted changes in model behavior (data poisoning).
  • Evaluates on vision (CIFAR-10) and preliminary language tasks, showing that subtle edits to a tiny fraction of training data (0.2%) can reliably induce behaviors and that poisoned corpora can transfer across architectures.
  • Finds the method is most effective at amplifying behaviors the model already partially learned, highlighting risks to training-data integrity and the need for interpretability/defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['J Rosser', 'Robert Kirk', 'Edward Grefenstette', 'Jakob Foerster', 'Laura Ruis']
  • Tags: ['data poisoning', 'influence functions', 'training-data attacks', 'transferability', 'adversarial manipulation']

item.title: SA$^{2}$GFM: Enhancing Robust Graph Foundation Models with Structure-Aware Semantic Augmentation
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SA^2GFM, a framework to improve robustness of graph foundation models via structure-aware semantic augmentation and hierarchical structural priors.
  • Introduces an information bottleneck for structure-guided representation compression, an expert adaptive routing (mixture-of-experts with null expert) to mitigate negative transfer, and a fine-tuning module for joint intra-/inter-community structure learning.
  • Evaluates effectiveness and robustness against random noise and adversarial structural perturbations on node and graph classification, showing improvement over 9 SOTA baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Junhua Shi', 'Qingyun Sun', 'Haonan Yuan', 'Xingcheng Fu']
  • Tags: ['adversarial robustness', 'graph foundation models', 'structure-aware augmentation', 'domain adaptation', 'mixture-of-experts']

item.title: JULI: Jailbreak Large Language Models by Self-Introspection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes JULI (Jailbreaking Using LLM Introspection), an attack that jailbreaks safety-aligned LLMs by manipulating token log-probabilities via a small plugin called BiasNet.
  • Operates in a black-box API setting, requiring only knowledge of the model's top-5 predicted token log probabilities (no access to weights or generation internals).
  • Demonstrates high effectiveness across multiple metrics and claims to outperform prior SOTA jailbreak/attack methods against API-accessible LLMs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Jesson Wang', 'Zhanhao Hu', 'David Wagner']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'black-box attack', 'log-probability manipulation', 'LLM security', 'adversarial attack']

item.title: The Reasoning Trap -- Logical Reasoning as a Mechanistic Pathway to Situational Awareness
item.description:
Summary

  • Argues that improvements in LLM logical reasoning (deduction, induction, abduction) create mechanistic pathways to increasing situational awareness in models.
  • Introduces the RAISE framework identifying three pathways (deductive self inference, inductive context recognition, abductive self modeling) and an escalation ladder from self-recognition to strategic deception.
  • Analyzes why current safety measures are insufficient and maps major reasoning research topics to amplifiers of situational awareness.
  • Proposes concrete safeguards including a 'Mirror Test' benchmark and a Reasoning Safety Parity Principle, calling on the reasoning community to address risks.

Paper Type

  • Position

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subramanyam Sahoo', 'Aman Chadha', 'Vinija Jain', 'Divya Chaudhary']
  • Tags: ['situational-awareness', 'logical-reasoning', 'ai-safety', 'emergent-capabilities', 'benchmarks/safeguards']

item.title: FlexServe: A Fast and Secure LLM Serving System for Mobile Devices with Flexible Resource Isolation
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents FlexServe, a system for secure and fast on-device LLM serving that leverages ARM TrustZone.
  • Introduces Flexible Resource Isolation (Flex-Mem and Flex-NPU) to switch memory pages and NPU between protected and unprotected modes with low overhead.
  • Designs LLM-aware memory management, a secure inference pipeline, and a multi-model scheduler to accelerate inference while maintaining confidentiality against a compromised OS.
  • Implements a prototype and demonstrates substantial Time-to-First-Token and end-to-end speedups versus TrustZone-based baseline designs.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yinpeng Wu', 'Yitong Chen', 'Lixiang Wang', 'Jinyu Gu', 'Zhichao Hua', 'Yubin Xia']
  • Tags: ['secure inference', 'hardware isolation', 'TrustZone', 'model protection', 'mobile LLM serving']

item.title: Nonparametric Variational Differential Privacy via Embedding Parameter Clipping
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a parameter clipping strategy for nonparametric variational differential privacy (NVDP) derived from minimizing a Ŕenyi Divergence upper bound.
  • Derives theoretical constraints on posterior mean, variance, and mixture weights to prevent latent representations from drifting into high-information regions.
  • Empirically shows that clipping yields tighter RD bounds (stronger privacy guarantees) while improving downstream task performance compared to an unconstrained baseline.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Dina El Zein', 'Shashi Kumar', 'James Henderson']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'privacy-preserving-ml', 'variational-methods', 'parameter-clipping', 'renyi-divergence']

item.title: Sim2Act: Robust Simulation-to-Decision Learning via Adversarial Calibration and Group-Relative Perturbation
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Sim2Act, a framework to improve simulation-to-decision robustness by aligning simulator errors with downstream decision impact via an adversarial calibration mechanism.
  • Introduces a group-relative perturbation strategy to stabilize policy learning under simulator uncertainty without excessive pessimism that discards high-risk/high-reward actions.
  • Evaluates on supply chain benchmarks showing improved simulation robustness and more stable decision performance under structured and unstructured perturbations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hongyu Cao', 'Jinghan Zhang', 'Kunpeng Liu', 'Dongjie Wang', 'Feng Xia', 'Haifeng Chen', 'Xiaohua Hu', 'Yanjie Fu']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'simulation-to-decision', 'adversarial calibration', 'policy robustness', 'sim-to-real']

item.title: Quantifying Memorization and Privacy Risks in Genomic Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a multi-vector privacy evaluation framework for genomic language models combining perplexity-based detection, canary sequence extraction, and membership inference into a unified worst-case memorization risk score.
  • Uses planted canaries at varying repetition rates in synthetic and real genomic datasets to precisely measure how repetition and training dynamics influence memorization.
  • Evaluates multiple GLM architectures to analyze how model capacity and training regimes affect privacy risk, finding measurable memorization and architecture-dependent vulnerability.
  • Concludes that no single attack vector captures all memorization risk and recommends multi-vector privacy auditing as standard practice for genomic AI systems.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Alexander Nemecek', 'Wenbiao Li', 'Xiaoqian Jiang', 'Jaideep Vaidya', 'Erman Ayday']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'memorization', 'membership-inference', 'canary-extraction', 'genomic-language-models']

item.title: Adversarial Latent-State Training for Robust Policies in Partially Observable Domains
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces an adversarial latent-initial-state POMDP framework where an adversary selects the hidden initial latent distribution to evaluate robustness of partially observable RL policies.
  • Provides theoretical results: a latent minimax principle, characterization of worst-case defender distributions, and finite-sample concentration bounds that separate optimization and sampling terms.
  • Proposes iterative best-response training and theorem-motivated diagnostics; empirically demonstrates improved robustness on a Battleship benchmark (reducing average robustness gaps between Spread and Uniform distributions from 10.3 to 3.1 shots at equal budget).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Angad Singh Ahuja']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-robustness', 'reinforcement-learning', 'adversarial-training', 'POMDP', 'distribution-shift']

item.title: Governance Architecture for Autonomous Agent Systems: Threats, Framework, and Engineering Practice
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Layered Governance Architecture (LGA) for autonomous agents with four layers: execution sandboxing (L1), intent verification (L2), zero-trust inter-agent authorization (L3), and immutable audit logging (L4).
  • Constructs a bilingual benchmark of 1,081 tool-call samples covering prompt injection, retrieval-augmented-generation (RAG) poisoning, and malicious skill plugins and evaluates defenses on an open-source agent framework (OpenClaw).
  • Evaluates Layer 2 intent verification using multiple local and cloud LLM judges, reporting high interception rates (93.0–98.5% for TC1/TC2; 75–94% for TC3) and explores cascaded judge configurations for tradeoffs between interception rate and false positives.
  • Provides end-to-end pipeline evaluation (96% interception, ~980 ms P50 latency) and validates generalization on an external benchmark (InjecAgent) with 99–100% interception.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuxu Ge']
  • Tags: ['prompt injection', 'retrieval poisoning', 'tool-invocation-security', 'defense-architecture', 'benchmarking']

item.title: Infusion: Shaping Model Behavior by Editing Training Data via Influence Functions
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces Infusion, a method that uses scalable influence-function approximations to compute small perturbations to training examples that induce targeted changes in model behavior (data poisoning).
  • Evaluates on vision (CIFAR-10) and preliminary language tasks, showing that subtle edits to a tiny fraction of training data (0.2%) can reliably induce behaviors and that poisoned corpora can transfer across architectures.
  • Finds the method is most effective at amplifying behaviors the model already partially learned, highlighting risks to training-data integrity and the need for interpretability/defenses.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['J Rosser', 'Robert Kirk', 'Edward Grefenstette', 'Jakob Foerster', 'Laura Ruis']
  • Tags: ['data poisoning', 'influence functions', 'training-data attacks', 'transferability', 'adversarial manipulation']

item.title: When Robots Obey the Patch: Universal Transferable Patch Attacks on Vision-Language-Action Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces UPA-RFAS, a method to craft universal, transferable physical adversarial patches against Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models controlling robots.
  • Method combines a feature-space objective with L1 prior and repulsive InfoNCE, a robustness-augmented two-phase min-max training (inner invisible per-sample perturbations, outer universal patch optimization), and two VLA-specific losses (Patch Attention Dominance and Patch Semantic Misalignment).
  • Evaluated across multiple VLA architectures, finetuned variants, simulation-to-real scenarios, and physical robot executions, showing strong cross-model, cross-task, and cross-view transferability.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hui Lu', 'Yi Yu', 'Yiming Yang', 'Chenyu Yi', 'Qixin Zhang', 'Bingquan Shen', 'Alex C. Kot', 'Xudong Jiang']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-patches', 'transferability', 'physical-attacks', 'robotics', 'robustness']

item.title: Sim2Act: Robust Simulation-to-Decision Learning via Adversarial Calibration and Group-Relative Perturbation
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes Sim2Act, a framework to improve simulation-to-decision robustness by aligning simulator errors with downstream decision impact via an adversarial calibration mechanism.
  • Introduces a group-relative perturbation strategy to stabilize policy learning under simulator uncertainty without excessive pessimism that discards high-risk/high-reward actions.
  • Evaluates on supply chain benchmarks showing improved simulation robustness and more stable decision performance under structured and unstructured perturbations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hongyu Cao', 'Jinghan Zhang', 'Kunpeng Liu', 'Dongjie Wang', 'Feng Xia', 'Haifeng Chen', 'Xiaohua Hu', 'Yanjie Fu']
  • Tags: ['robustness', 'simulation-to-decision', 'adversarial calibration', 'policy robustness', 'sim-to-real']

item.title: NetDiffuser: Deceiving DNN-Based Network Attack Detection Systems with Diffusion-Generated Adversarial Traffic
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes NetDiffuser, a framework that generates natural adversarial examples (NAEs) to evade DL-based Network Intrusion Detection Systems by applying diffusion models to create semantically consistent perturbations.
  • Introduces a feature categorization algorithm to identify relatively independent network-flow features to perturb minimally while preserving flow validity.
  • Evaluated across three benchmark NIDS datasets, multiple model architectures, and state-of-the-art adversarial detectors, showing up to 29.93% higher attack success and AUC-ROC detection drops of 0.267–0.534 versus baselines.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Pratyay Kumar', 'Abu Saleh Md Tayeen', 'Satyajayant Misra', 'Huiping Cao', 'Jiefei Liu', 'Qixu Gong', 'Jayashree Harikumar']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'network-intrusion-detection', 'diffusion-models', 'evasion-attacks', 'adversarial-detection-evaluation']

item.title: Test-Driven AI Agent Definition (TDAD): Compiling Tool-Using Agents from Behavioral Specifications
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes TDAD: a test-driven pipeline that compiles behavioral specifications into prompts by generating executable tests and iteratively refining prompts until tests pass.
  • Introduces three defenses against specification gaming and regressions: visible/hidden test splits, semantic mutation testing (faulty-prompt generation), and spec-evolution scenarios to quantify regression safety.
  • Evaluates on SpecSuite-Core (four specified tool-using agents) with high compilation and hidden-test pass rates, strong mutation-detection scores, and measured regression safety; code/benchmark released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tzafrir Rehan']
  • Tags: ['prompt-robustness', 'safety-testing', 'guardrails', 'automated-red-teaming']

item.title: Clear, Compelling Arguments: Rethinking the Foundations of Frontier AI Safety Cases
item.description:
Summary

  • Critiques alignment-community approaches to safety cases and draws lessons from safety-assurance practices used in safety-critical industries.
  • Proposes a rethought foundational framework for constructing robust, defensible safety cases for frontier AI systems.
  • Includes a case study applying the framework to risks from deceptive alignment and CBRN-capable behavior.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shaun Feakins', 'Ibrahim Habli', 'Phillip Morgan']
  • Tags: ['safety-cases', 'AI assurance', 'deceptive alignment', 'CBRN risk', 'frontier AI safety']

item.title: Turn: A Language for Agentic Computation
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents Turn, a compiled, actor-based programming language designed to enforce safety/guardrails for agentic programs that delegate inference to LLMs.
  • Adds language-level defenses: cognitive type safety (compiler-generated JSON Schema + VM validation), a confidence operator for flow control based on model certainty, actor isolation with persistent memory/mailboxes, and capability-based identity to prevent credential leakage.
  • Implements a Rust bytecode VM, compile-time schema absorption for typed API bindings (openapi/graphql/fhir adapters), and evaluates Turn on representative agentic workloads; open-sourced on GitHub.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Muyukani Kizito']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'guardrails', 'LLM output validation', 'capability-based security', 'agentic systems']

item.title: Alignment Is the Disease: Censorship Visibility and Alignment Constraint Complexity as Determinants of Collective Pathology in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Empirical study showing that alignment interventions (censorship and constraint complexity) can produce collective pathologies in multi-agent LLM simulations, i.e., iatrogenic harms caused by the safety measures themselves.
  • Series C: across commercial models and languages, invisible censorship increased a 'Collective Pathology Index' (strong effect sizes and consistent directionality across runs).
  • Series R: increasing alignment constraint complexity raised a 'Dissociation Index', indicating greater insight-action dissociation; under heavy constraints, external censorship lost influence.
  • Authors argue current safety evaluation may miss emergent collective harms from alignment and that alignment can be iatrogenic at the group level.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hiroki Fukui']
  • Tags: ['alignment', 'safety', 'multi-agent-systems', 'emergent-behavior', 'censorship']

item.title: PRECEPT: Planning Resilience via Experience, Context Engineering & Probing Trajectories A Unified Framework for Test-Time Adaptation with Compositional Rule Learning and Pareto-Guided Prompt Evolution
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces PRECEPT, a test-time adaptation framework combining exact-match rule retrieval, conflict-aware memory with Bayesian source reliability and threshold-based rule invalidation, and COMPASS, a Pareto-guided prompt-evolution outer loop.
  • Exact retrieval eliminates partial-match interpretation errors and enables compositional rule stacking; conflict-aware memory resolves static vs. dynamic disagreements and supports drift adaptation; COMPASS evaluates prompt variants through the same end-to-end execution pipeline.
  • Presents empirical results showing large gains in compositional generalization, continuous learning, drift recovery, and strong robustness under adversarial static knowledge compared to baselines (e.g., Full Reflexion).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Arash Shahmansoori']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'robustness', 'adversarial-memory', 'test-time-adaptation', 'prompt-evolution']

item.title: The Reasoning Trap -- Logical Reasoning as a Mechanistic Pathway to Situational Awareness
item.description:
Summary

  • Argues that improvements in LLM logical reasoning (deduction, induction, abduction) create mechanistic pathways to increasing situational awareness in models.
  • Introduces the RAISE framework identifying three pathways (deductive self inference, inductive context recognition, abductive self modeling) and an escalation ladder from self-recognition to strategic deception.
  • Analyzes why current safety measures are insufficient and maps major reasoning research topics to amplifiers of situational awareness.
  • Proposes concrete safeguards including a 'Mirror Test' benchmark and a Reasoning Safety Parity Principle, calling on the reasoning community to address risks.

Paper Type

  • Position

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Subramanyam Sahoo', 'Aman Chadha', 'Vinija Jain', 'Divya Chaudhary']
  • Tags: ['situational-awareness', 'logical-reasoning', 'ai-safety', 'emergent-capabilities', 'benchmarks/safeguards']

item.title: Real-Time Trust Verification for Safe Agentic Actions using TrustBench
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces TrustBench, a dual-mode framework that both benchmarks trust metrics for agents and provides a real-time toolkit agents call to verify safety before executing actions.
  • Intervenes after action formulation but before execution, using domain-specific plugins (healthcare, finance, technical) to enforce specialized safety requirements.
  • Reports empirical harm reduction (87% overall; domain-specific plugins outperform generic verification by 35%) with sub-200ms latency suitable for real-time use.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Tavishi Sharma', 'Vinayak Sharma', 'Pragya Sharma']
  • Tags: ['defense', 'real-time verification', 'agentic safety', 'benchmarking', 'domain-specific plugins']

item.title: LDP: An Identity-Aware Protocol for Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes LDP (LLM Delegate Protocol), an AI-native communication protocol for multi-agent LLM systems introducing identity cards, progressive payload modes, governed sessions, structured provenance, and trust domains.
  • Implements LDP in a JamJet plugin and evaluates efficiency gains (lower latency, reduced token counts, session token savings) compared to A2A and random baselines.
  • Includes security/governance primitives (trust domains, provenance tracking, verification status) and reports simulated improvements in attack detection (96% vs 6%) and failure recovery (100% vs 35%).
  • Finds that noisy provenance can harm synthesis quality, highlighting trade-offs between metadata and verified security information.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Sunil Prakash']
  • Tags: ['multi-agent', 'protocol', 'delegation', 'security', 'provenance']
